item.message: Exceeded skills context budget of 2%. All skill descriptions were removed and 7 additional skills were not included in the model-visible skills list.

item.text: I’ll build this as a self-contained browser game in the candidate directory and avoid the ThreeNative tooling entirely, per the benchmark condition. I’m first checking the local files and the relevant Three.js guidance so the implementation fits the environment.

Using the `threejs-fundamentals` skill because this is a plain Three.js scene with camera, renderer, meshes, and animation. The directory currently only has the prompt/log artifacts, so I’ll add the browser files without touching anything outside it.

I’m going with 2D browser canvas rather than pulling in external Three.js packages. That keeps the benchmark artifact static and self-contained while still giving readable launcher, projectile, targets, floor, score, retry, and collision feedback.

The first pass is in place. I’m validating it now in two layers: a quick JavaScript syntax check, then a real browser load with simulated keyboard input so the score/progression path is actually reachable.

item.text: Because valid throws only count after the previous ball fully stops, reaching the end state takes longer than needed for verification. I’m adding an explicit keyboard retry: Enter resets the round at any time, while Space remains launch/retry at game-over.
The retry route is now immediate and keyboard-driven: Enter resets the game from any state. I’m checking that syntax still passes, then I’ll confirm the HUD resets in the loaded browser page.
Syntax is clean. I’m reloading once more so the browser has the new retry code, then I’ll perform Space to score and Enter to reset.
